title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents elevate software engineering from code-writing to system design. Engineering principles like decomposition, state management, and systems thinking remain essential but apply at a higher level of abstraction when building agentic systems.
writeup: Angie Jones makes the case that AI coding agents shift the engineer's job from writing code to designing systems. The argument holds that core engineering principles, including decomposition, separation of concerns, state and context management, and systems thinking, still apply, but at a higher level of abstraction when building agentic systems. The takeaway: the value moves from authoring lines of code to structuring how agents and their components fit together.
